text: Two operations run through this history. Commensuration makes heterogeneous flows comparable on a common metric -- a grant-equivalent percentage, a marker coefficient, a dollar total [@espeland_stevens1998]. Economisation is the further step by which the resulting quantity becomes an economic object in its own right: given a perimeter and a target, made to grow, and governed by the reasoning of leverage and mobilisation [@caliskan_callon2009]. The same device often performs both, the second move riding on the first. That passage, traced period by period, is the thread running through the rest of this article.
source: manuscript.qmd @sec-intro l.70 (ticket 0243 sample 1 — MOOT: superseded by the merged full pass, kept as a historical pair)

text: Two operations run through this history. The first is commensuration: putting unlike flows on one scale, a grant-equivalent percentage, a marker coefficient, a dollar total [@espeland_stevens1998]. The second is economisation: the quantity so produced becomes an economic object in its own right, with a perimeter, a target, and a doctrine of leverage and mobilisation to make it grow [@caliskan_callon2009]. One device often does both jobs, the second riding on the first. The rest of this article follows that passage, period by period.
source: manuscript.qmd @sec-intro l.70 (ticket 0243 sample 1 — MOOT: superseded by the merged full pass, kept as a historical pair)

text: A parallel infrastructure emerged within the UNFCCC process. The Standing Committee on Finance, established at COP16 in Cancún (2010), was tasked with producing biennial assessments of climate finance flows. Where the DAC system was designed by and for donors, reporting what they had provided, the UNFCCC process was also accountable to recipient countries, which reported what they had received, and the two figures rarely agreed. The first Biennial Assessment (2014) estimated total flows for 2011--2012 at $340--650 billion per year, and allowed they might be higher still -- a range so wide that it illustrated the problem more than it resolved it [@unfccc2014biennial]. Two measurement regimes, each embedded in a different political constituency, were producing different numbers from the same underlying reality. In this battle for the operational definition, the OECD's figures became the de facto reference for assessing progress towards the $100 billion target, because the DAC produced data that was more standardised and more timely. The development-economics categories embedded in the DAC system won the definitional contest through infrastructural incumbency, not intellectual superiority.
source: manuscript.qmd @sec-crystallization l.134 (ticket 0243 sample 2 — ACCEPTED, applied with an author-directed content fix)

text: A parallel infrastructure emerged within the UNFCCC process. The Standing Committee on Finance, established at COP16 in Cancún (2010), was tasked with producing biennial assessments of climate finance flows. The DAC system was built by and for donors: it counted what they had provided. The UNFCCC process also answered to recipient countries, which counted what they had received. The two figures rarely agreed. The first Biennial Assessment (2014) put total flows for 2011--2012 at $340--650 billion per year, and allowed they might be higher still; a range that wide illustrated the problem more than it resolved it [@unfccc2014biennial]. Two measurement regimes, each answering to its own constituency, produced different numbers from the same reality. The OECD's figures became the de facto reference for progress towards the $100 billion target, because the DAC delivered data that was more standardised and more timely. The development-economics categories embedded in the DAC system won the definitional contest the way incumbents usually do: the machinery was already running.
source: manuscript.qmd @sec-crystallization l.134 (ticket 0243 sample 2 — ACCEPTED, applied with an author-directed content fix)

text: The periodization -- 1990--2006, 2007--2014, 2015--2024 -- rests first on institutional history: before Bali and Copenhagen the category is not yet settled; between 2007 and 2014 the counting devices crystallise; after Paris the controversies unfold within an established frame. To test it without whispering the answer, the break years are sought while ignoring the calendar of the COPs. For each candidate year between 2005 and 2023 we compare the thematic composition of the years just before and just after, along two complementary measures: the Jensen--Shannon divergence between the theme-share vectors, which catches redistribution across themes, and the cosine distance between mean embeddings, which catches drift in the continuous space of meaning. Each year's score is expressed as a z-score, so that the two measures share one scale, and the comparison is run at three window widths of two, three and four years.
source: manuscript.qmd Appendix A.4 l.280 (ticket 0243 sample 3 — AMENDED and applied, several rounds)

text: The periodization -- 1990--2006, 2007--2014, 2015--2024 -- rests first on institutional history: before Bali and Copenhagen the category is not yet settled; between 2007 and 2014 the counting devices crystallise; after Paris the controversies unfold within an established frame. The test must not whisper the answer, so we search for the break years while ignoring the calendar of the COPs. For each candidate year between 2005 and 2023 we compare the thematic composition of the years just before and just after. Two measures complement each other: the Jensen--Shannon divergence between theme-share vectors catches redistribution across themes; the cosine distance between mean embeddings catches drift in the continuous space of meaning. Each year's score is expressed as a z-score, so the two measures share one scale, and the comparison is run at three window widths of two, three and four years.
